name: HTS_SCHED_VIOLATION_MSG_TKNS
description: This table is associated with a specific violation message and represents the typed message tokens that were used to format it the violation message.

columns.Name: SCHED_VIOLATION_MSG_TKN_ID
columns.Comments: The unique ID of the validation message token.

columns.Name: SCHED_VIOLATION_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the associated validation violation whose error message this token is contributing to.

columns.Name: SCHED_FULL_VALIDATION_ID
columns.Comments: The validation this error message token belongs to.

columns.Name: TOKEN_NAME
columns.Comments: The name used in the message format as placeholder for the token value.

columns.Name: TOKEN_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: The code identifying both the type of the token value and the way to format it, so to translate it as a string.

columns.Name: TOKEN_DATA_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: A code indicating if the token value is a number, string or date.

columns.Name: TOKEN_NUMBER_VALUE
columns.Comments: This column stores the token value for the number token types.

columns.Name: TOKEN_LOOKUP_TYPE
columns.Comments:
This column stores the value's lookup type for any
token that is a LOOKUP_CODE

columns.Name: TOKEN_STRING_VALUE
columns.Comments: This column stores the token value for the string token types.

columns.Name: TOKEN_DATE_VALUE
columns.Comments: This column stores the token value for the date token types.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
